item.title: China’s Eve Energy Signs Battery Deal With India’s Godawari
item.description: China’s Eve Energy Co. agreed to supply India’s Godawari New Energy Private Ltd. with large-scale batteries, as a rapid renewable power expansion drives demand for energy storage in the South Asian nation.

item.title: Tech Rout Deepens India Stock Slump as Austerity Steps Kick Off
item.description: A recovery in tech shares may be slow, Bajaj Broking says.

item.title: Japan’s Current Account Surplus Hit Record High in March
item.description: Japan’s current account surplus rose to the highest on record, supported by a widening trade and income surplus, with higher oil prices expected to push it lower in coming months.

item.title: India’s Diesel Crunch Leaves Truckers Stranded in Roadside Lines
item.description: At a service station outside Nagpur, in the geographic heart of India, nearly 30 trucks are lined up along either side of the highway, carrying everything from garments to packed snacks to Amazon packages and even railway equipment.

item.title: New BHP CEO Brandon Craig Says Miner Open to Deals for Growth
item.description: BHP Group’s incoming Chief Executive Officer Brandon Craig has not ruled out dealmaking as a key part of the mining giant’s growth strategy.

item.title: BOJ’s Policy Rate Likely to Reach 2% by End of 2027, OECD Says
item.description: The Bank of Japan’s policy rate is expected to reach 2% by the end of 2027, according to a new estimate from the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development.

item.title: Memory Shortage Deepens Divide Between Stock Winners and Losers
item.description: The worsening shortage in global memory chips due to the artificial intelligence buildout is driving a widening gulf in corporate results and stock performances.

item.title: India More Than Doubles Gold, Silver Tariffs to Defend Rupee
item.description: India has raised import tariffs on gold and silver in an attempt to defend its currency, a surprise move as the country races to limit the damage from the Middle East war and to shore up foreign-exchange reserves.

item.title: Japan’s 20-Year Bond Yield Rises to 1997 High on Inflation Woes
item.description: Japan’s 20-year government bond yield breached its January peak to touch the highest level since 1997, as elevated energy prices add to inflation pressure.

item.title: NZ Can’t Rely on Geographic Isolation for Security, Luxon Says
item.description: New Zealand can no longer assume its isolated location and “quiet reputation” will protect it and must instead boost its national resilience and security to navigate volatile geopolitics, Prime Minister Christopher Luxon said.

item.title: Korean Stocks Rebound as Retail Traders Counter Foreign Selling
item.description: South Korean stocks rapidly erased losses Wednesday as local retail investors bought into a wave of selling by foreign funds.

item.title: India’s Economic Strength Defies Foreign Outflow Fears, S&P Says
item.description: India is weathering global financial pressures better than headline data suggest, with worries over foreign outflows overstated, according to S&P Global Ratings.

item.title: Shiseido Dips Most in 6 Months on Weak Sales Despite Profit Beat
item.description: Shiseido Co. shares fell the most in nearly six months after the Japanese cosmetics company reported first-quarter net sales that just missed analyst estimates.

item.title: Australian Budget to Support Debt, Hit Consumer Stocks: Analysts
item.description: Australian bonds may find longer-term support after an initial selloff, while consumer-linked shares may come under pressure following tax changes unveiled in the fiscal 2027 budget, analysts said.

item.title: Short-Seller Left ‘Didn’t Make Much Sense,’ Cronos CEO Testifies
item.description: The chief executive officer of a Canadian cannabis distributor told jurors at the US criminal trial of Citron Research founder Andrew Left he was stunned when the prominent short-seller issued a report saying the company was overvalued, prompting shares to plunge.

item.title: Indonesia Vows ‘Smart Interventions’ as Rupiah Falls
item.description: Indonesia’s central bank pledged “smart interventions” in the foreign-exchange markets as the rupiah fell to a record low.

item.title: Trump Tower Plan for Australia’s Gold Coast Scrapped
item.description: The Trump Organization has scrapped plans to put its name to a tower on Australia’s Gold Coast, about three months after announcing a deal to bring the brand’s first hotel to the country.

item.title: Philippine Bonds Face Extended Slump on Large Rate Hike Bets
item.description: Philippine sovereign bonds are extending a selloff as traders price in the prospect of a 50-basis-point rate hike, the largest since 2023.

item.title: Geothermal Energy Developer Fervo Raises $1.89 Billion in US IPO
item.description: Fervo Energy Co., a geothermal energy developer, raised $1.89 billion in a US initial public offering that priced above the marketed range, after upsizing the deal earlier this week.

item.title: Nidec Drops 18% After Flagging Quality Conduct Concerns
item.description: Nidec Corp. shares slumped as much as 18% in Tokyo on Wednesday after the company said it identified suspected improper conduct on product quality.

item.title: Alibaba, Tencent AI Bets Face ‘Show Me the Profits’ Scrutiny
item.description: Chinese investors are heading into the earnings season demanding proof that billions in AI spending by tech giants is paying off.

item.title: Hedge Fund CFM Opens Office in Shanghai, Assets Top $27 Billion
item.description: French quantitative hedge fund Capital Fund Management has opened an office in Shanghai, following in the footsteps of peers in establishing a presence in the world’s second-largest economy.

item.title: China’s AI Suppliers Can’t Keep Up as Component Shortages Bite
item.description: China’s artificial intelligence hardware suppliers have a problem: keeping up with insatiable demand for their products.

item.title: AI Life Science Firm Metis TechBio Set for HK Debut After IPO
item.description: Metis TechBio Co, a biotechnology company using artificial intelligence to deliver and formulate drugs, is set to begin trading in Hong Kong after raising HK$2.1 billion ($270 million) in an initial public offering.

item.title: Gold Holds Decline as US Inflation Jump Lowers Rate Cut Odds
item.description: Gold was steady after US data showed an acceleration in inflation that lowers the odds of the Federal Reserve reducing interest rates this year.

item.title: Goldman Sees Dollar Strength as Energy Shock to Keep Rates High
item.description: The dollar strength will build further over the near term as the energy-price shock will keep yields elevated as economic growth remains relatively resilient, according to Goldman Sachs Group.

item.title: AI Chipmaker Cerebras to Guide IPO Pricing Above Range
item.description: Cerebras Systems Inc. is guiding prospective investors that it expects to price its initial public offering above the top of its marketed range, according to people familiar with the matter, as demand soars for the chipmaker’s stock.

item.title: Asian Stocks Under Pressure as US Inflation Rises: Markets Wrap
item.description: Asian stocks are poised for a weak open after losses on Wall Street, with the dollar strengthening and bonds falling as inflation data showed the impact of energy disruptions linked to the war in Iran.

item.title: Oil Holds Gain With Iran’s Exports Strained as Conflict Drags On
item.description: Oil was steady as the conflict in the Middle East drags on and keeps the vital Strait of Hormuz effectively closed, with shipments from Iran’s main export terminal appearing to come to a standstill.

item.title: Hedge Fund Dymon’s Assets Near $8 Billion, Blowing Past Estimate
item.description: Hedge fund firm Dymon Asia Capital Pte is on track to reach $8 billion in assets under management by the third quarter as more global investors seek to back Asia-based hedge funds, according to people familiar with the matter.
